text: All of the following produce neurotransmission-associated neurotoxicity except _____.
A domoic acid exposure in Canada caused a neurological syndrome because domoic acid is an analog of _____.
All of the following are true of excitatory neurotransmitters except _____.

opts.A: Their toxicity has been implicated in neurodegenerative disease.
opts.B: Glutamate is the main excitatory neurotransmitter of the brain.
opts.C: Kainate is 100 times more potent than glutamate.
opts.D: Benzodiazepines block the excitatory amino acid receptor.

text: A meperidine derivative that is neurotoxic is _____.
A common toxicologic mechanism for chemicals causing Parkinson’s disease is _____.
The neurotoxic metabolite of MPTP _____.

opts.A: crosses the blood-brain barrier by active transport
opts.B: is formed in astrocytes by MAO-B

text: All of the following are developmental neurotoxicants except _____.
Toxicant-induced amyotrophic lateral sclerosis has been caused by _____.
Bilirubin and hexachlorophene are most toxic to a/an _____.
amantadine
clarithromycin
beta-adrenergic blockers
carbon monoxide
meperidine metabolite
lovastatin
barbiturates
salicylates
The first site of action for the eye after exposure to a toxicant is the _____.
All of the following statements are true regarding the corneal stroma except _____.

opts.A: It makes up 90 % of the corneal thickness.
opts.C: It can act as a reservoir for toxicants.
opts.D: It is composed of water, collagen, and glycosaminoglycans.

text: A systemic water-soluble toxicant orally ingested would have the least exposure to the _____.
Electrophysiologic procedures used to test the effects of toxicants on visual function include all of the following except _____.
Chemical toxicity is usually associated with what type of color vision deficit?
With respect to surfactant damage to the cornea, which of the following orders of damage are true?
Organic solvents splashed into the eye should be treated with _____.

opts.A: five milliliters of olive oil irrigation followed by water irrigation
opts.B: irrigation with one drop of dish detergent in one gallon of water

text: All of the following are true of the lens except _____.

opts.A: It has a dual blood supply.
opts.B: It grows over the life span of an individual.
opts.C: It is metabolically active and maintains an ionic balance.
opts.D: It is composed of approximately two-thirds water and one-third protein.

text: The mammalian retina is very vulnerable to toxicant-induced damage for all of the following reasons except _____.

opts.A: a high rate of mitochondrial metabolism
opts.B: a low choroidal blood flow rate
opts.C: high turnover of rod and cone outer segments

text: All of the following are true regarding rodent retina except _____.

opts.A: Its toxicology has relevance for toxicant exposure during the early gestation period in humans.
opts.C: There is no melanin in the choroid.
opts.D: Rat rods are similar to those in humans and other primates.

text: Degenerative changes in the optic nerve can result from deficiencies in all of the following except _____.
An antituberculosis drug associated with eye toxicity is _____.
Solvents that selectively damage the optic nerve and tract are _____.
A chemical used to assess corneal damage is _____.
The slit lamp microscope is least useful in evaluating the _____.
Acute angle closure glaucoma can be precipitated by _____.
Inorganic lead exposure has been associated with all of the following except _____.
The area of the brain associated with vision is _____.
An area of the central nervous system with a weakened blood-brain barrier is _____.
Intraocular melanin has a high-binding affinity for _____.
The most severe damage to the cornea results from contact with _____.
Cataracts have been associated with all of the following except _____.
Which of the following drugs are toxic to the retina?
Digoxin is toxic to the _____.
The eye toxicity of methanol results from _____.
Perchloroethylene eye toxicity has been noted in residents living close to _____.
Styrene has been associated with _____.
Which of the following can compromise the stratum corneum barrier and lead to increased penetration of chemicals?
Which of the following skin areas has the highest permeability?
All of the following are true regarding absorption through the epidermal appendages except _____.

opts.A: Chemicals can bypass the stratum corneum by this route.
opts.B: It is more relevant for chronic rather than acute situations.
opts.C: It is usually neglected for most chemicals because of the smaller relative surface compared to the stratum corneum.
opts.D: In some cases like benzopyrene, absorption through appendages can be primary.

text: Which of the following agents has the highest skin penetration under normal conditions?

opts.A: hydrophilic chemicals of high molecular weight
opts.B: hydrophobic chemicals of high molecular weight
opts.C: hydrophilic chemicals of low molecular weight
opts.D: hydrophobic chemicals of low molecular weight

text: All of the following are advantages of transdermal drug delivery except _____.

opts.A: a steady infusion rate over long-time intervals
opts.B: variation of infusion rate with body temperature

text: Which of the following statements is true regarding systemic exposure through the skin while swimming in chemically contaminated water?

opts.A: Exposure can occur for some chemicals.
opts.B: Significant exposure can only occur by swallowing the water.
opts.C: Significant exposure can only occur through the nasal mucous.
opts.D: There are no reported cases of significant human exposure by this route.
